item.title: Hiring 5 more SDRs long (2026-04-17)
item.description:
"We're hiring 5 more SDRs."
I heard this twice this week. The companies actually pulling ahead are doing the opposite.
Last 90 days: .. Block cut 40% of its team. Profit guidance up 54%. .. Salesforce cut support headcount almost in half. Agentforce hit $500M ARR in one year. .. Klarna: 5,500 to 3,400 employees. AI doing the work of 700 agents.
36% of B2B companies cut their SDR team in 2025. Highest cut rate of any sales role.
The shift isn't "fire everyone." It's "stop backfilling. Let AI absorb the repetitive work. Keep humans on closing conversations."
If you're adding headcount to solve a pipeline problem in 2026, you're solving it with last decade's tools.
What's the smallest team that could run your entire sales motion today?
